name: Bockwurst
recipeInstructions:
1. Use veal shoulder or trimmings.
2. Work the veal through a fine blade grinder and add it to a large bowl.
3. Sprinkle chives, salt, sage, white pepper, sugar, mace, cloves, and lemon extract over the meat.
4. Add eggs and cream; beat until the mixture comes away from the sides of the bowl (beat 5 minutes).
5. Fill small hog casings with the mixture and tie or twist into pieces measuring about 6 inches each.
6. Bring a large pot of heavily salted water to a boil.
7. Lower the sausages, preferably in a wire basket, into the boiling water and simmer very gently for 2-3 minutes.
8. Let cool in the water, then drain.
9. Store refrigerated for up to 2 days.
10. To serve: simmer the sausages again in water for 10-15 minutes, drain, and serve with bread and butter seasoned with sweet mustard.
(Note: Saltpeter is an optional preservative available at drugstores; casings can be found at specialty meat markets.)
